assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states 'Classification: code change request' and 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' report.md table row: '30001 | Code change request | Sub-task created (transaction wrapping)'. subtask-30001.md exists with full sub-task description.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'Classification: suggestion' and explains reasoning: '"should also" is additive and suggestive', '"would help" is conditional/suggestive language', '"Something like:" proposes an example approach rather than prescribing a specific fix'.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states 'Classification: nit' and 'No sub-task created. Minor style feedback that does not affect correctness.' No subtask-30003.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states 'Classification: question' and 'No sub-task created. This asks for clarification; no code change is requested.' No subtask-30004.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (trustify-backend), '## Description' (transaction wrapping), '## Files to Modify' (sbom/service/sbom.rs), '## Implementation Notes' (4 bullet points), '## Acceptance Criteria' (4 checkboxes).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section with URL 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' section with Comment ID 30001, Author reviewer-a, File modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs:60, and the full original comment text about transaction wrapping.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All files in outputs/ are .md report/classification files: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 review comments have classification files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md. report.md Review Comment Classifications table lists all 4 with classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents findings with 'Overall: WARN' and 'Summary of issues requiring attention' for human review. No merge action, merge command, or auto-merge language appears anywhere in the report.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file with 5 test functions covering deletion (204), non-existent (404), already-deleted (409), include_deleted query, and cascade verification. No test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a full 'Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section covering CONVENTIONS.md check ('No CONVENTIONS.md file exists'), codebase pattern check ('No other migration files are present in the diff'), and conclusion ('does NOT qualify for convention upgrade'). report.md Style/Conventions section also states: 'Comment 30002 (index suggestion) examined for upgrade eligibility.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states 'No sub-task created. This is a valid suggestion for the PR author to consider, but it does not meet the threshold for a tracked code change request.' report.md table: '30002 | Suggestion | No sub-task (suggestive language, no convention backing)'. No subtask-30002.md file exists.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: 'Eval Quality: N/A (no eval result reviews detected -- the 3-criteria detection for author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals found no matches).' Detailed findings section repeats: 'No eval result reviews detected. The 3-criteria detection ... found no matches. Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination.'
